translated: He said to the people of the village, this is what he said: "Ti-Dindaan has said that we should go and see the people of the village." He said this to Simeon, who then said: "Ti-Dindaan has died."

translated: The-Dindaan Unimbɔti went to the forest and saw a big tree, and saw a lot of fruit on the tree and the fruit was ripe, and he picked some fruit.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created the world and everything in it, and he created everything in the world. He also created humans and animals.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: They say that the rabbit and the tortoise were friends, that the rabbit and the tortoise were friends, that they and the rabbit's younger brother were friends with the tortoise, but one day the rabbit's mother said she would prepare a meal.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his son to the mountain, and when they reached the top of the mountain, he took the knife to slaughter his son, but God sent his angel to stop him and he was given a sheep to slaughter instead.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The people who were in the village heard the sound of the drum and said, "Jacob has killed the lion that was eating our goats in the village, and we will go to the forest, and we will see the lion that was eating our goats."

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: It is Jacob who is in the house. He says that Unimboti is going to the farm to dig up peanuts.

text: Isimaayeel naantiibi nìn kóò kí yòon̄ Afiila tiŋki kí tin cáá sāā Suur tiŋki ní. Suur nìn bí ki pìlīǹ Eejipiti tiŋki, ní ki cá Asuur tiŋki watil pú. Niin ní bi nìn kóò. Bi nì bi-nabiyaamu Abraam maaliŋun yab bin kíǹee kaa nìn kpí. JAAKƆB (
translated: Ishmael and his mother went and dwelled near a well of water, as they came from the direction of Beersheba. Beersheba is near the border of Egypt, and it is on the way to Assyria. That is where they dwelled. That is where the well of Abraham's servant's well is, the one they dug and found water.

ref: The two angels said to Lot, “The Lord has heard many terrible things about the people of Sodom, and he has sent us here to destroy the city. Take your family and leave. Take every relative you have in the city, as well as the men your daughters are going to marry.”
text: Di sá yii ti làá kúntí kitiŋ nee, kun puee ti-Dindaan nín gbìl̄ bi sòoǹ ki-kpitii pu miŋyee wīikí tikpil, ní u túnní-ti yii tí dāá kúntí-kì.»
translated: The day that the chief died, the people of Dindaan heard the sound of a gun and ran to the chief's house, but when they arrived, the chief was already dead.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Á cù kuŋɔcaŋkpuŋun kí lēeń iŋɔpɔɔn ili kí cáań-mi ḿ ŋá tijin tin a-baa néeńee
translated: The child is crying because he fell and scraped his knee on a rock outside

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lootan jipɔmbi di sá Oori nì Eemam ní u-niisal sá Tiimina.
translated: The people of Oori and Eemam will meet in Tiimina.

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Abram nimpuu Saarayi kaa nìn màl̄ kibiki. U nìn cáá uyumbusapɔɔn ubɔ, u sá Eejipiti tiŋkin bi yíì-u yii Agaar.
translated: Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all their possessions which they had gathered, and the persons that they had acquired in Haran; and they set forth to go into the land of Canaan; but when they came into the land of Canaan, Abram passed through the land unto the place of Shechem, unto the terebinth of Moreh. ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his son to the mountain, and when they reached the top of the mountain, he took the knife to slaughter his son, but God sent his angel to stop him and he was given a sheep to slaughter instead.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The people who were in the village heard the sound of the drum and said, "Jacob has caught a big animal in his trap in the bush near the village and has gone to call the people of the village to come and help him carry it, and they have gone to carry the big animal that he caught.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: It is Jacob who is in the house. He says that Unimboti has given him a book.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb tīī Eesayuu kpɔnɔ nì atùmman kunti, ní u ŋmɔ́ ki wūfì tikun, ní ki yíkìi búntì. Eesayuu nín ŋá min ki kpēē ki sīŋkiǹ u-kpitee dee.
translated: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and pottage of lentils; and he ate and drank, and rose up, and went his way: thus Esau despised his birthright.

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Boonee ní u yíkì niin ki búntì Beerseeba.
translated: The well is located between Kadesh and Bered, near Beersheba.

ref: She was seen by Hamor's son Shechem, the leader of the Hivites, and he grabbed her and raped her.
text: Ní Amɔɔr un sá Eefi tiŋki bɔtiuee jipɔɔn Siikeem ká-u ní ki cúú-u mpɔn ki dōoǹ nì u ki bíil̀-u.
translated: The chief of Amor went to Eefi's land and took Siikeem's yam and he ate it and he drank palm wine and he became drunk.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Abram nìn sá ugaajakpaan ní. U nìn cáá tiwaŋkuti tikpil ki cáá animbil nì sindaariya.
translated: Abraham is our ancestor. He is the father of our tribe and the father of many nations.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Ní Laaban kíí Jaakɔb yii: «Binimpoobi nee sá man bisalib ní, ní bi-biyaamu sá man biyaamu. Ipii nee sá man piii ní, tin kɔkɔ a pée wāĺ nee sá man wanti ní. Díǹ pu nee, ba ní m làá ŋá kí tīī m-bisalib nì bi-biyaamu mun bi màl̄ee?
translated: Laban said to Jacob: "The speckled shall be your wages, and the spotted shall be your wages. The striped shall be your wages, and all the brown ones among the sheep shall be your wages. Would that it might be so, if he would give me the speckled among the goats and the spotted among the goats, all of them."

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created the world and everything in it, and he created everything in the world of God. He made the rivers flow in the world.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: It is Jacob who is in the house. He says that Unimboti has given him a book.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created the world and everything in it, and he created everything in the world of God. He made the rivers flow in the world.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: They say that the rabbit and the tortoise were friends, that the rabbit and the tortoise were friends, that they and the rabbit's younger brother were friends with the tortoise, but one day the rabbit's mother said she would prepare a meal.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his son to the mountain, and when they reached the top of the mountain, he took the knife to slaughter his son, but God sent his angel to stop him and he was given a sheep to slaughter instead.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
